title: Do not add mount options for VFS when privileged
body:
when running a container in privileged mode, no mount options should be
included for the vfs filesystem.

Signed-off-by: baude <bbaude@redhat.com>

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *baude* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Flibpod). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [baude] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
@cevich Mind rebasing on top of this? Think this is our fix the the mount options problems on F30
NO NO NO. Lets stop screwing around here.  What is the reason for this?  If the configuration file is wrong, the user needs to clean this up.

For the VFS graph driver, c/storage eats the default mount options - it just ignores them.

We accidentally worked around this with our code for removing noexec/nodev/nosuid for privileged containers - c/storage is ignoring the options, but we're adding them back explicitly, so it lets us do this and then errors out (again, VFS only).

Non-privileged containers work fine with the VFS driver and mount options set, because c/storage is smart enough to know that options aren't supported and handle that.

(Potential alternative to this: we could patch c/storage to return an empty list of default mount options through `storage.GetDefaultMountOptions()` if it's ignoring them - that's what's hitting us here, the mount options that c/storage says are the default are not actually the ones in use)

For posterity, here's an exact reproducer:
```
# grep ^mountopt /etc/containers/storage.conf
mountopt = "nodev,metacopy=on"
# CONMON_BINARY=/path/to/hand-built/conmon-v0.2.0
# /path/to/podman --conmon $CONMON_BINARY --root=/tmp/ttt --storage-driver=vfs run --privileged alpine date
Error: error mounting storage for container c08c2552b43977ff516e87e0f939c4cf58eeb75b4060c9567b2811b3baa48490: vfs driver does not support mount options
```
Explanation:
* `mountopt` gained `metacopy=on` between skopeo-0.1.36-8.dev.gitd93a581.fc30 and **-9** (actually containers-common, which is a subpackage of skopeo). Prior to -9, it was only `nodev`, which the code addressed by this PR would filter out.
* conmon v0.2.0 is needed by current podman master. Grumble grumble.
* The `--privileged` option is required; I cannot reproduce the bug without it. Thanks to whomever discovered that!

Given all that: yes, this PR addresses and resolves the problem. (I will not venture an opinion on whether this is the best possible such fix).

IMO, if the user overrides `--storage-driver` then it should also set `--storage-opts`.

`--storage-driver=vfs` should have the same effect as `--storage-driver=vfs --storage-opts=""`
@giuseppe I think that already happens; it's only `--privileged` that has special-case code that restores it back from the storage.conf settings. See @mheon's comment above.

I don't think it's is that does that, though; it's c/storage ignoring them
(selectively, I think) when a non default graph driver is selected

Doesn't my patch still fix this other issue @edsantiago?

@rhatdan sorry, I should've said that in my comment. No, #3240 addresses another issue, but not actually the one in question here. See my comment above, the one with the reproducer -- probably because of the weird special-case handling of `--privileged`. It's one of those finicky situations where lots of things need to align _just so_; that's why I posted my reproducer, because it took me a long time to actually get it right.
Ok I found the bug
https://github.com/containers/storage/pull/362

When you run podman --privileged we were ignoring the user specified options that overrode the mountoptions and instead using the systemdefault mountoptions. In --privileged mode we are looking to remove the nodev flag on images, but since we used the systemdefaults the metacopy=up option was leaking through.

I have opened PRs in containers/storage and updated my PR to fix this.  BTW This is a worse bug then originally thought, since --privileged would ignore User Input for mount options.

if you guys think this is resolved please close this as Im on PTO.  That said, I'm wondering if the real root cause here is that mount options are defined "globally" in that config and it is assumed they apply to all FSs, which for VFS it cannot.  And this is done by default.  IMHO if a user chooses to override on the commandline for vfs, no default option in storage.conf should break that.  can mount options be defined in the fs stanzas?

> can mount options be defined in the fs stanzas?

They can (see `[storage.options.thinpool]`) and, imho, they should.
Closing this PR, since I think my PR addresses this issue now.
I was thinking the same thing :wink: but there's no way I'm adding any new tests, or doing anything at all, until #3290 and #2947 are committed and running.

hmmm, jeepers, what's holding those up?
